channel.title: theBalancedBriefNLhealthAndWellness
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from theBalancedBriefNLhealthAndWellness, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Kenya-Uganda trial reduces HIV incidence by 70% in rural populations
item.description: By pairing digital tools with tailored HIV services delivered by community health workers and clinicians, a study has reduced new HIV cases by 70% in ...

item.title: Ultrasound gives the brain a nudge in the right direction
item.description: Neuroscientist Soha Farboud of the Donders Institute at Radboud University has succeeded in adjusting activity in specific brain areas using a new tec...

item.title: A human mini-bladder shows the culprit of recurrent infections
item.description: Researchers at EPFL, Heidelberg University and Roche have built a human mini-bladder to show how urine composition weakens bladder tissue, helping inf...

item.title: Lab-grown reservoir cells aim at HIV's last strongholds
item.description: A new study has overcome a long-standing challenge: how to isolate and study elusive HIV-infected cells called authentic reservoir clones (ARCs) that ...

item.title: RHR: The Gut-Brain Connection: How Your Microbiome Affects Your Mental Health
item.description: Depression and anxiety are often treated as chemical imbalances in the brainâbut what if the real problem starts in the gut? In this episode of Revolu...

item.title: Could a gene lower nicotine dependence? What a CHRNB3 variant suggests
item.description: Variants in a nicotine receptor gene are associated with a lower likelihood of heavy smoking, according to a study published in Nature Communications....

item.title: RHR: Vitamin D: Why a Personalized Approach Is Best
item.description: In this episode of Revolution Health Radio, Chris Kresser explains why vitamin D supplementation should never be one-size-fits-all. Drawing on new res...

item.title: RHR: Cold Exposure: Why Sustained Beats Extreme
item.description: Cold plunges and ice baths dominate social media, but do they really deliver the best metabolic benefits? In this episode of Revolution Health Radio, ...

item.title: RHR: Why Your Sleep Schedule Matters As Much As Duration
item.description: In this episode of Revolution Health Radio, Chris challenges the conventional focus on sleep duration and makes the case that sleep timing and circadi...

item.title: RHR: Menopause Brain FogâWhatâs Really Happening (and How To Fix It)
item.description: In this episode of Revolution Health Radio, Chris examines research confirming that menopause-related brain fog is real, measurable, and reversible. H...
